message:
chore(beads): file flywheel_connectors-on7yd (fcp-host dispatcher test-gap follow-up)

Adds a single new open bead tracking three test-gap regression
coverage items identified in a fresh-eyes review of commit 58e25d0e
(the fcp-host subprocess RPC dispatcher hardening).

The dispatcher tests in crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs around
`subprocess_connector_queue_saturation_returns_error` (~line 6433)
and `subprocess_connector_queues_concurrent_health_requests` only
exercise queue-saturation (capacity=1 filling) + happy-path
concurrency. Missing race-window coverage:

1. Fast-request-not-blocked-by-slow-request: spawn slow health() +
   fast configure() concurrently, verify configure completes first
   (independent request handling through the dispatcher).
2. response_rx dropped before dispatcher sends: drop the oneshot
   receiver before the dispatcher can reply, verify no panic and no
   poison propagation into the next request.
3. Dispatcher task panic/poison: simulate a handler panic, verify
   the poison flag prevents reuse and the next request gets a
   clean error.

Priority 1 / issue_type: task. Not a bug in the dispatcher itself
(commits e6f125b3 and 5f4e8b60 were clean per adversarial review);
this is regression-prevention coverage for future changes to the
dispatcher path.

No existing beads are closed, reopened, or otherwise modified.
